item.title: Fine-tuning Segement Anything (SAM) for Mastcam-Z Mission Operations
item.description: In Fall 2023, I fine-tuned the Segment Anything Model (SAM) to produce robust terrain segmentation masks for Mars 2020 Rover imagery in Mastcam-Z and Navcam images to support enhanced image stitching and spectral contrast in a Unity based observation planning tool used by the Mastcam-Z Operations Engineers and Scientists called Viewpoint. The fine-tuned model has been deployed since fall 2023 without requiring retraining to date (July 2025), producing segmentation masks upon image downlink to support daily operations shifts. Fine-tuning used pytorch and lightning on a A-100 80GB gpu with the huggingface implementation of SAM and Weights and Biases for experiment tracking.

item.title: Mineralogy, morphology, and emplacement history of the Maaz formation on the Jezero crater floor from orbital and rover observations
The Complex Exhumation History of Jezero Crater Floor Unit and its implication for Mars Sample return
Three-dimensional data preparation and immersive mission-spanning visualization and analysis of Mars 2020 Mastcam-Z stereo image sequences
Spectral variability of rocks and soils on the Jezero crater floor: A summary of multispectral observations from Perseverance’s Mastcam-Z instrument
